steps.title: Target the formal platform
steps.content: The underlined claim has two parts: the exhibition amplified the security personnel's own perspective, and that perspective was not usually given a formal platform. A supporting quote must therefore show an individual guard's viewpoint becoming part of the curated show after previously existing only outside formal museum presentation.

steps.title: Rule out weaker quotes
steps.content: Choice A says the show is unique and expresses affection for the protected works, but it does not show a previously informal viewpoint receiving a platform. Choice B focuses on lesser-known artifacts, not on the curator's personal perspective. Choice D emphasizes help from professional museum staff, so it does not best support the claim about security personnel's distinct perspective.

steps.title: Match the personal viewpoint
steps.content: Choice C describes a statement of personal interpretation developed through hours of guarding the work and interacting with visitors. It also says that interpretation had previously been shared only in small impromptu conversations, which directly supports the idea that the exhibition gave a formal platform to a perspective that had not usually received one. The answer is C.
